# Standards — Bank-Kata
# Generated by Anthara on 2026-03-12
# DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY — changes will be overwritten on next deploy.

## Org-wide Standards

<!-- anthara:set:1:start -->
### Engineering Standards

- **Error Handling**
  Use typed error classes with centralized error boundaries.
  ```
  throw new AppError("NOT_FOUND", "Patient not found");
  ```

- **Database Access**
  Use repository pattern with parameterized queries.
  ```
  const patient = await patientRepository.findById(id);
  ```

- **Logging**
  Use structured logging with correlation IDs; use safeLog() for PHI-adjacent contexts.
  ```
  logger.info({ correlationId, action: "patient.read" });
  ```

- **API Response Format**
  Use standardized envelope format (status, data, error, pagination).
  ```
  { "status": "success", "data": { ... }, "pagination": { ... } }
  ```

- **Authentication**
  Use JWT with short-lived access tokens and refresh token rotation.

- **Input Validation**
  Validate all inputs at API boundary using schema validation (Zod, Joi).
  ```
  const validated = schema.parse(req.body);
  ```

- **Environment Config**
  Use environment variables for all configuration; never use defaults for secrets.

- **Date/Time**
  Use UTC for all storage and server-side logic; convert to local time only at display layer.

- **Naming Conventions**
  Use camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for types/classes, SCREAMING_SNAKE for constants.
<!-- anthara:set:1:end -->

<!-- anthara:set:2:start -->
### PHI Data Access Patterns

- **PHI Read Access**
  Access PHI through dedicated data access service with mandatory audit logging.
  ```
  const data = await phiService.read(patientId, { auditReason });
  ```

- **PHI Write Access**
  All PHI mutations go through validated write service with before/after audit trail.

- **PHI Redaction**
  Use field-level redaction utility before logging or external transmission.
  ```
  const safe = redact(patient, ["ssn", "dob"]);
  ```

- **PHI Encryption**
  Use AES-256 for PHI at rest; use application-level encryption for sensitive fields.

- **PHI Minimum Necessary**
  Only query PHI fields actually needed for the use case (select specific columns).

- **PHI Display**
  Mask PHI in UI by default; require explicit user action to reveal full values.

- **PHI Export**
  All PHI exports (CSV, PDF, API) must go through the audit-logged export service.
<!-- anthara:set:2:end -->
